text: Good my friends,

text: And of his friends there, it is lots to blanks

speaker: MENENIUS
text: My name hath touched your ears. It is Menenius.

speaker: MENENIUS
text: I tell thee, fellow,

text: Thy general is my lover. I have been

text: The book of his good acts, whence men have read

speaker: MENENIUS
text: His fame unparalleled happily amplified;

speaker: MENENIUS
text: For I have ever verified my friends—

text: Of whom he’s chief—with all the size that verity

speaker: MENENIUS
text: Would without lapsing suffer. Nay, sometimes,

speaker: MENENIUS
text: Like to a bowl upon a subtle ground,

speaker: MENENIUS
text: I have tumbled past the throw, and in his praise

speaker: MENENIUS
text: Have almost stamped the leasing. Therefore, fellow,

speaker: MENENIUS
text: I must have leave to pass.

              WATCH
text: Faith, sir, if you had told as many lies in

text: to lie as to live chastely. Therefore, go back.

text: Prithee, fellow, remember my name is Menenius,

speaker: MENENIUS
text: always factionary on the party of your

text: general.

text: Has he dined, can’st thou tell? For I would

speaker: MENENIUS
text: not speak with him till after dinner.

FIRST
               
              WATCH
text: You are a Roman, are you?

text: I am, as thy general is.

text: Then you should hate Rome as he does.

text: Nay, but fellow, fellow—

text: say an errand for you. You shall know now that I

text: am in estimation; you shall perceive that a Jack

text: Guess but by my entertainment with him

what’s to come upon thee.

               The

speaker: MENENIUS
text: glorious gods sit in hourly synod about thy particular

speaker: MENENIUS
text: prosperity and love thee no worse than thy old

speaker: MENENIUS
text: father Menenius does! O my son, my son!

speaker: MENENIUS
text: Thou art preparing fire for us; look thee,

speaker: MENENIUS
text: here’s water to quench it. I was hardly moved to

speaker: MENENIUS
text: come to thee; but being assured none but myself

speaker: MENENIUS
text: could move thee, I have been blown out of your

speaker: MENENIUS
text: gates with sighs, and conjure thee to pardon Rome

speaker: MENENIUS
text: and thy petitionary countrymen. The good gods

speaker: MENENIUS
text: assuage thy wrath and turn the dregs of it upon

speaker: MENENIUS
text: this varlet here, this, who, like a block, hath denied

speaker: MENENIUS
text: my access to thee.

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Away!

speaker: MENENIUS
text: How? Away?

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Wife, mother, child, I know not. My affairs

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Are servanted to others. Though I owe

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: My revenge properly, my remission lies

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: In Volscian breasts. That we have been familiar,

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Ingrate forgetfulness shall poison rather

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Than pity note how much. Therefore, begone.

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Mine ears against your suits are stronger than

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Your gates against my force. Yet, for I loved thee,

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Take this along; I writ it for thy sake,

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: And would have sent it. Another word, Menenius,

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: I will not hear thee speak.—This man, Aufidius,

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Was my beloved in Rome; yet thou behold’st.

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: You keep a constant temper.
